definition: A tendency that remains lying underneath experience and can reassert itself when conditions support it.
notes: The project keeps underlying tendency as the default because it is plain English that still preserves the doctrinal image of something lying underneath. Latent tendency is a controlled alternate when dormancy needs to be foregrounded, but anusaya should not be reduced to mere habit or personality. In this repository, anusaya is not the same as actively obstructive nīvaraṇas, not the same as the binding saṃyojana set, and not the same as the broader kilesa family. It names a layer of latent persistence that may not be manifest on the surface yet still conditions reaction. Formula and compound handling should preserve that dormant-but-potent force in cases such as rāgānusaya, diṭṭhānusaya, and mānānusaya. The OSF glossary supports keeping underlying tendency as the house default, and MN 64 together with SN 22.89 support the family logic of tendencies that lie underneath and can be uprooted rather than merely suppressed.

context_rules.context: In doctrinal analysis of latent persistence and reactivity
In explanatory prose emphasizing dormancy or hidden persistence
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when the hidden aspect needs extra emphasis.

context_rules.context: In translator-facing explanation where the skewing or leaning quality needs to be audible
context_rules.notes: Use selectively and only when bias reads more naturally than tendency.

context_rules.context: In verbal or formulaic contexts built on anuseti
context_rules.notes: Keep the noun family stable rather than flattening the term into everyday habit-language.

example_phrases.notes: Shows the verbal sense behind the doctrinal noun.
Shows a named latent residue rather than an active surface state.
Connects the residue family to the already-stabilized identity-construction cluster.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping underlying tendency as the house default while preserving latent tendency as a controlled alternate.
Supports the verbal and compound logic of tendencies that lie underneath and can be uprooted.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'underlying tendency' as the default rendering in the entry's governed doctrinal contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'latent tendency' or 'underlying bias' only when the explanatory context clearly needs them. Do not flatten anusaya into mere habit, instinct, or personality-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between latent residue, active hindrance, binding fetter, and broad defilement-language.
